query_normalized: evaluating the impact of data scaling on statistical test sensitivity statistics
rationale: The paper focuses on the convergence rates and robustness of distributed optimization algorithms (SGD) under Byzantine attacks, which is a machine learning systems problem, rather than evaluating the statistical power or sensitivity of hypothesis tests as a function of data scaling. While it mentions "statistical heterogeneous data," it does not measure the relationship between sample size (data scaling) and the ability to detect effects (test sensitivity), failing to meet any of the inclusion c
